question_text: How would doubling the mass of a planet affect the gravitational field strength at its surface?

options.label: A
options.text: It would have no effect because gravity depends only on distance

options.label: B
options.text: It would approximately double the gravitational field strength

options.label: C
options.text: It would reduce the gravitational field strength

options.label: D
options.text: It would quadruple the gravitational field strength

correct_answer: It would approximately double the gravitational field strength
explanation: Gravitational field strength (g) is calculated using the formula g = GM/r², where G is the gravitational constant, M is the planet's mass, and r is the distance from the centre. Since g is directly proportional to mass, if you double the mass, the gravitational field strength also doubles. The distance (radius) stays the same, so only the mass change affects the result.
